title: rhinanthus major subsp. glaber
intro: Rhinanthus major subsp. glaber, also known as the hairy or glabrous yellow rattle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family. This parasitic plant is known to have medicinal properties, specifically its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate digestive issues due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rhinanthus major subsp. glaber include its potential to treat diarrhea, dyspepsia, and other gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. Rhinanthus major subsp. glaber can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health issues.
